title: eriophyllum jepsonii
intro: Eriophyllum jepsonii, also known as Jepson's woolly sunflower, is a perennial shrubby plant that belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae) and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Eriophyllum jepsonii include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Eriophyllum jepsonii can be prepared as an herbal remedy by making a tea from its leaves and flowers, or by using it in salves and ointments to apply topically to affected areas.
